key_insight: This survey frames LLMs as a new interface layer for VRP, where they help translate requirements, generate solution components, and coordinate tools for constrained routing optimization.
engineering_relevance: The paper is directly relevant to logistics, transportation scheduling, and industrial automation because it connects natural-language task specification with practical vehicle routing solvers.
method: Maps LLM roles in VRP into three functions: modelers, designers, and coordinators.
Covers VRP variants, solver families, and the LLM concepts needed to apply them.
Reviews benchmarks, real-world datasets, evaluation frameworks, and two comparative experiments.
evidence_signal: The survey draws on standard benchmarks, real or near-real operational datasets, LLM-oriented evaluation frameworks, and two comparative experiments to assess progress.
watch_item: LLM-generated routing solutions still need careful constraint checking and external solver validation, especially for NP-hard VRP variants with complex operational rules.
paper_title: Vehicle Routing Problem Meets Large Language Models: An Overview and Perspectives
